name: Skopje
description: Capital and largest city of North Macedonia, featuring the iconic Stone Bridge and Macedonia Square

name: Ohrid
description: Historic lakeside city and UNESCO World Heritage site with ancient churches and fortifications

name: Bitola
description: City of consuls, known for its Ottoman architecture and Shirok Sokak pedestrian street

name: Tetovo
description: Northwestern city known for the colorful Painted Mosque (Šarena Džamija)

name: Lake Ohrid
description: One of the oldest and deepest lakes in Europe, shared with Albania

name: Matka Canyon
description: Stunning canyon and artificial lake just 15km from Skopje, popular for hiking and kayaking

name: Heraclea Lyncestis
description: Ancient Greek and Roman archaeological site near Bitola with well-preserved mosaics

name: Stone Bridge
description: Historic Ottoman stone bridge connecting Old Bazaar with Macedonia Square in Skopje

name: Kale Fortress
description: Medieval fortress overlooking Skopje, offering panoramic views of the city

name: Mavrovo National Park
description: Largest national park in North Macedonia, featuring mountains, lakes, and traditional villages

name: Kokino Observatory
description: Ancient megalithic observatory from the Bronze Age, one of the oldest in the world

name: Tikvesh Wine Region
description: Famous wine region in central Macedonia known for excellent vineyards and wine tours
